evals.prompt: I want a skill that reminds the agent to use kebab-case for all new file names in our frontend repo. It should trigger when someone is creating or renaming files in src/. No code generation, just a reminder.
evals.expected_output: A Tier-1 skill folder at dist/kebab-case-filenames/ (or similar slug) containing only SKILL.md with a trigger-rich description naming kebab-case, file naming, and renaming files; a 'When to use' section listing realistic triggers; a 'When not to use' section that excludes content files like docs/markdown; a 'Behavior' section explaining the rule in one short paragraph.

assertions.text: Output folder contains SKILL.md and no other directories.
SKILL.md description includes the words 'kebab-case' or 'naming' or 'rename'.
validators/validate_skill.py exits 0 on the produced folder.

evals.prompt: We need a skill that guides a multi-step design review: structure, tokens, components, accessibility, and a final summary. Knowledge lives in our existing design-system-rules.md. Should auto-trigger when someone asks for a 'design review' or mentions 'review my Figma'.
evals.expected_output: A Tier-3 skill folder containing SKILL.md, references/ (with at least rules.md referencing the existing design-system-rules.md), and workflows/ (default.md, plus one or more variant files matching the named review steps). SKILL.md description mentions design review, Figma review, design audit.

assertions.text: Output folder contains references/ and workflows/ directories.
workflows/ contains at least one file with a numbered procedure of 3 or more steps.
SKILL.md description mentions both 'design review' and a Figma-related trigger phrase.
validators/validate_skill.py exits 0 on the produced folder.

evals.prompt: Build a skill that converts a JSON token export (in Figma's design-tokens format) to a CSS variables file. It should validate the input against a schema and warn on unknown token types. Triggers on phrases like 'export figma tokens to css', 'turn tokens.json into variables', etc.
evals.expected_output: A Tier-4 skill folder with SKILL.md, references/, workflows/ (generate.md and validate.md or similar), scripts/ (generate.py and validate.py with real argparse and structured exit codes — NOT placeholder bodies), and evals/ (trigger-evals.json with at least one positive and one negative case, plus output-evals.json).

assertions.text: Output folder contains scripts/ and evals/ directories.
scripts/generate.py and scripts/validate.py both use argparse and have exit codes documented in their docstrings.
evals/trigger-evals.json contains both positive and negative cases.
SKILL.md references ai/implementation-principles.md or restates the relevant principles.
validators/validate_skill.py exits 0 on the produced folder.
